title: Enforce Complete Assertions
description: It is important to write complete assertions to ensure that tests verify the intended results. This rule enforces complete assertions in the following cases: Variable assignments and return statements are skipped to allow helper methods.
recommendation: Write complete assertions that verify the intended results.
impact: Incomplete assertions can lead to undetected issues in the code.

checks.message: Variable assignments and return statements should not be considered assertions.
Assertions must be complete and verify the intended results.
Assertions must have at least one argument to be considered complete.
